Applied_Promoters.reason: The paper states genes for tobacco transient expression were driven by the M24 promoter of Mirabilis mosaic virus. Yeast expression used episomal vectors in galactose media, but PAM itself was not described as expressed in yeast reconstruction; β-phenylalanine was fed exogenously in yeast.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Nicotiana benthamiana is explicitly used for PAM assays. Saccharomyces cerevisiae EGY48 is the yeast host used in the study, but PAM expression there is not stated.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: N. benthamiana is used without variety specification, and yeast strain EGY48 is named. But PAM deployment in yeast is not established.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: PAM was confirmed to make β-phenylalanine in tobacco and was sufficient to drive N-debenzoyl-2′-deoxy-taxol formation there, supporting its value as an upstream module. Core_Validation_Method.reason: Supported by transient expression in tobacco and UPLC–qTOF–MS confirmation of β-phenylalanine, plus pathway reconstruction evidence.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Agroinfiltration in tobacco and exogenous baccatin III/10-deacetyl-baccatin III feeding are described. Yeast 20°C conditions are in the paper, but PAM was not deployed in yeast there.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Directly supported: exogenous β-phenylalanine did not increase N-debenzoyl-2′-deoxy-taxol levels when PAM was present in tobacco.
